title: 'A cigarette or love for Maccies' can help CSI track down burglars

input.lead: CSI Amber Boswell says finding a stolen can reveal lots of forensic evidence as "the gloves are off"
input.summary: CSI Amber Boswell says finding a stolen can reveal lots of forensic evidence as "the gloves are off"
input.evidence_text: 'A cigarette or love for Maccies' can help CSI track down burglars - BBC News Image caption, Crime Scene Investigator, Amber Boswell, gathers forensic evidence to help catch criminals By Annie Knowlson North West Published 1 hour ago On a cloudy morning, Crime Scene Investigator (CSI) Amber Boswell is searching for clues following a burglary. The 26-year-old is part of a team from Greater Manchester Police who collect forensic evidence, which is used to bring criminals to court - and she deals with a range of crimes, from vehicle theft to murder. She looks for fingerprints and footprints from crime scenes and extracts marks from tools used by burglars, which can later help identify repeat offenders. Boswell said recovering a stolen car could lead to vital evidence. "The gloves are off, they might have a cigarette, they love a Maccies drive through," she said. "Which gives us a lot more forensic potential." In July 2021, GMP increased their use of CSIs by launching Operation Castle - pledging to send an officer to every reported domestic burglary. The force said this effort is seeing continued successes in the reduction of burglaries. 'Violation of privacy' BBC North West Tonight joined Boswell, as she worked on a call-out to a family home in northern Manchester. The family car was stolen after four offenders broke into their home and found the keys. Boswell said whenever she visits a burgled residential property she begins her work by checking through bins, looking around the outside of the house and speaking to the family affected. "It's a massive violation of privacy," she explained. "We like to think that we're making a massive difference in helping people move on from being a victim of crime and providing some support." title: Mahmood says law will be changed to help deport grooming gang leader

input.lead: Calls to deport Rochdale grooming gang ringleader Shabir Ahmed have been growing since his release from prison
input.summary: Calls to deport Rochdale grooming gang ringleader Shabir Ahmed have been growing since his release from prison
input.evidence_text: Calls have mounted for the deportation of Shabir Ahmed, who had dual British-Pakistani citizenship before being stripped of the former following his 2012 conviction for multiple counts of child sexual offences Ahmed, who was jailed for 22 years and released on licence this month, cannot be deported due to a 1971 law forbidding the removal of a small group of Commonwealth citizens who arrived in the UK more than 50 years ago Under the proposed amendment, foreign criminals will no longer benefit from these protections where they are guilty of some serious crimes It would bring the law on deportation in line with the law on the removal of citizenship Mahmood said the 1971 Act provided protections for long-term UK residents but "should not be used as a bar against removal in cases like that of Shabir Ahmed" But the Home Office said Ahmed's removal depends on Pakistan accepting him. At the moment, it appears Pakistan has no intention of accepting Ahmed, who claims to have renounced his Pakistani citizenship

final_reason: Status: drop

title: Burnham cements Labour leadership with backing of 349 MPs

input.lead: The MP for Makerfield is on track to become the prime minister next week after receiving the support of the vast majority of Labour MPs
input.summary: The MP for Makerfield is on track to become the prime minister next week after receiving the support of the vast majority of Labour MPs
input.evidence_text: After a second day of nominations, the MP for Makerfield received the support of a further 27 Labour MPs - putting him on track to become prime minister on 20 July, with it now mathematically impossible for a rival to run against him He still requires the backing of three organisations affiliated to the Labour Party, at least two of which must be trade unions, although this is expected to be a formality The former mayor of Greater Manchester fielded questions from Labour MPs as the only participant in an online hustings on Monday evening It marks an extraordinary rise to power for a man who returned to Westminster only three weeks ago following his historic by-election win in Makerfield In a statement on Thursday after receiving the backing of 322 MPs on the first day of nominations, Burnham said support for him had come from across the party and reflected a "shared belief that Britain needs a new approach to politics" "That is the circuit breaker I am offering: power out of Westminster, an economy rewired for ordinary people, and good growth in every postcode," Burnham said

title: Woman's death in fire at Rochdale farm no longer murder inquiry

input.lead: A man was initially held on suspicion of killing the 38-year-old, whose body was found in stables
input.summary: A man was initially held on suspicion of killing the 38-year-old, whose body was found in stables
input.evidence_text: Woman's death in fire at Rochdale farm no longer murder inquiry - BBC News Image caption, Detectives now believe there was no "third-party involvement" in the woman's death By Angela Ferguson North West Published 13 July 2026 A woman's death in a fire at a farm in Greater Manchester is no longer being treated as a murder investigation, police have said. A man was arrested on suspicion of killing the 38-year-old , whose body was found inside stables at Higher Tunshill Farm in Milnrow, Rochdale, on 3 July. But in an update issued on Monday, Greater Manchester Police said detectives now believe there was no "third-party involvement" in her death. The arrested man, aged in his 40s, was released with no further action. "The circumstances surrounding the death will now be referred to the coroner and an inquest will take place in due course," the force said. title: Twelve arrested over threat to an Islamic event in Suffolk

input.lead: Secretary Shabana Mahmood says the police's response "undoubtedly saved lives"
input.summary: Secretary Shabana Mahmood says the police's response "undoubtedly saved lives"
input.evidence_text: Twelve arrested over threat to an Islamic event in Suffolk - BBC News Image source, Shaun Whitmore/BBC Image caption, A police presence remains at the scene at Shrubland Hall, at Barham, in Suffolk By Alice Cunningham Suffolk Published 13 July 2026 Twelve people have been arrested over an alleged right-wing terror threat to an Islamic event at a country estate in Suffolk, police said. Counter Terrorism Policing (CTP) said the UK Ijtima event at Shrubland Hall at Barham was ended early on Sunday as a precaution after the possible threat. Home Secretary Shabana Mahmood said the response of the police in tackling a "credible threat" to the festival had "undoubtedly saved lives". Commander Helen Flanagan, head of CTP London, said officers moved quickly to make the arrests across various locations in the country. Image source, Shaun Whitmore/BBC Image caption, Thousands of people are understood to have attended the UK Ijtima in Suffolk at the weekend CTP confirmed the investigation was "right-wing terrorism related" and officers were conducting searches at various addresses across the country linked to those arrested. It said eight men were arrested and detained under section 41 of the Terrorism Act 2000 and remain in custody. Three other men were arrested on suspicion of conspiracy to murder and a woman was arrested on suspicion of assisting an offender. Of those, one person has since been released on bail, while the other three remain in custody. Police confirmed once they had been made aware of a "potential serious threat" toward the event it had declared a major incident, but that status had since been stood down. The following arrests were made around the country: Five men, aged 27, 29, 55, 60 and 82 were arrested in Surrey A 35-year-old man was arrested in Greater Manc

title: Why voting method change matters in mayoral poll

input.lead: Why the Supplementary Vote may allow voters to vote with their heads and their hearts
input.summary: Why the Supplementary Vote may allow voters to vote with their heads and their hearts
input.evidence_text: The Labour Party could benefit most from the return of the Supplementary Votes (SV) system in the Greater Manchester mayoral race, a polling expert has said Voters in the region head to the polls on 30 July and will be able to select a first and second choice of candidate as SV comes into replace the first-past-the-post (FPTP) system used at the last election A number of candidates have already declared for the contest , triggered after Andy Burnham's successful campaign to become the MP of Makerfield, leaving the post he has held since 2017 vacant Prof Jonathan Tonge of the University of Liverpool said Labour could pick up second preference votes from Greens and Lib Dems, while Reform could be less likely to see Restore voters put them as second preference due to "bad blood" between the party leaders The system was used in the first two mayoral elections, but was scrapped by the last Conservative government in favour of FPTP system, before it was reintroduced again by Labour Under SCV, candidates need to win more than 50% of the vote in the first round to be come mayor or the top two candidates go into a run-off where second preference votes from eliminated candidates are counted

title: What is the Greater Manchester mayor and what do they do?

input.lead: Greater Manchester goes to the polls on 30 July, but what powers does the region's mayor hold?
input.summary: Greater Manchester goes to the polls on 30 July, but what powers does the region's mayor hold?
input.evidence_text: Andy Burnham's victory in the Makerfield by-election means he has to step down as Greater Manchester mayor Burnham has been mayor since the role was first created in 2017 and the race to become his successor will be fiercely contested by all involved But what is the role they will be fighting for? The role of mayor was created as part of a law passed in 2016, one of a number of big pieces of devolution legislation brought in by the Conservatives after taking power in 2010 In a 2012 referendum, Manchester rejected having a directly-elected city mayor, along with eight other major cities, but the decision to have a regional or metropolitan mayor was directly negotiated between the government and the 10 Greater Manchester councils Andy Burnham, who left Parliament to stand for the role, was chosen as Labour's candidate for the first mayoral election in 2017 and won, taking 63.4% of the vote

title: How do I register to vote in the May 2026 elections?

input.lead: Here’s what you need to know about how you register to vote and when
input.summary: Here’s what you need to know about how you register to vote and when
input.evidence_text: Click to expand How do I register to vote? Millions of voters across England, Scotland and Wales are heading to the polls on 7 May. Depending where you are, you might be voting to elect your local council members, a new mayor, members of the Welsh Senedd, or Scottish Parliament But if you're going to vote, you need to make sure you are registered. The deadline for registering to vote has passed but there's still time to apply for a postal or proxy vote Here's what you need to know about how and when you register to vote Registering only takes a few minutes if you do it online. It’s the same process whether you plan to vote in person at a polling station, by post, or through a proxy voter Go to the gov.uk website and fill out the form. You'll need your National Insurance number and current address details to hand If you can't provide your NI number you can still complete the online form but you'll need to provide documents like a passport or utility bill to your local electoral registration office before you're properly registered

title: Man killed in Ashton-under-Lyne karaoke bar was 'gentle giant'

input.lead: Shane Cullen, 31, was found dead at Blues bar on Bow Street, Ashton-under-Lyne on Saturday morning
input.summary: Shane Cullen, 31, was found dead at Blues bar on Bow Street, Ashton-under-Lyne on Saturday morning
input.evidence_text: Man killed in Ashton-under-Lyne karaoke bar was 'gentle giant' - BBC News Image source, GMP Image caption, Shane Cullen's family say he was a "gentle giant" and he would be "sadly missed" Published 13 July 2026 The family of a man who was killed in a "violent and shocking" attack at a karaoke bar have said their "hearts are broken". Shane Cullen, 31, died at Blues bar in Ashton-under-Lyne at about 02:40 BST on Saturday, Greater Manchester Police said. He was found with serious injuries and was pronounced dead at the scene. A 36-year-old man has been arrested on suspicion of murder. In a tribute, Cullen's mother said: "Shane was a big, gentle giant who wouldn't hurt anyone." "He was a good dad, son and brother. He will be sadly missed," she said. "All our hearts are broken and can never be fixed. "Shane would have a smile to light up the room." Their family was "broken" and could "never be put back together", his mother added. Image caption, Police were called to reports of an assault at Blues bar on Bow Street, Ashton-under-Lyne, in the early hours of the morning Det Supt Ian McNabb said: "This is was a violent and shocking attack that tragically took the life of a young man." He appealed for any witnesses or anyone with "footage from the area that may support our enquiries" to come forwards. Blues on Bow Street is a cabaret and karaoke bar which opened in February. It opens five nights a week with cabaret and drag acts as well as karaoke. title: Major disruption to Metrolink services for £150m upgrade works

input.lead: There are no trams operating on the Eccles, Trafford Centre and Altrincham lines for three weeks
input.summary: There are no trams operating on the Eccles, Trafford Centre and Altrincham lines for three weeks
input.evidence_text: Trams services in Greater Manchester are out of action on three lines for the next three weeks as part of a major £150m upgrade Tracks are being replaced between Trafford Bar, Cornbrook and Deansgate-Castlefield with the work starting on 13 July and running until 2 August Transport for Greater Manchester (TfGM) said no trams would operate on the Eccles, Trafford Centre and Altrincham lines while the East Didsbury/Manchester Airport lines would terminate at Firswood and the Rochdale line would terminate at Exchange Square Replacement buses are running between affected stops during improvement work TfGM said work would also take place at Eccles and the city centre around Piccadilly Gardens during this closure to help to reduce the need for further disruptions to the network A special event bus service will be in place for The Hundred returning to the Old Trafford Cricket Ground on 26 July and also on 31 July

title: Chester Zoo summit aims to tackle 'misleading' weather apps

input.lead: The zoo says rain icons lead some would-be visitors to believe the whole day will be a "washout"
input.summary: The zoo says rain icons lead some would-be visitors to believe the whole day will be a "washout"
input.evidence_text: Chester Zoo is hosting a summit to try and tackle misleading weather apps costing it up to £137,000 per day in lost visitor income It launched a campaign to tackle the problem in March, when chief operating officer Dom Strange said the sight of a single rain icon - which could mean a brief shower was forecast - was deterring some visitors "Since then, we've had constructive conversations with the Met Office and this summit is the next step," he said The zoo, which is holding the summit with the Met Office, government officials and 16 major UK attractions, wants a move to a Norwegian-style, four-slot forecast, splitting the day into six-hour windows rather than one summary icon "Getting government and 16 of the country's biggest attractions round the same table is a real opportunity," Strange said "We want to leave this room with recommendations we can actually put into practice."

The role of mayor was created as part of a law passed in 2016, one of a number of big pieces of devolution legislation brought in by the Conservatives after taking power in 2010 In a 2012 referendum, Manchester rejected having a directly-elected city mayor, along with eight other major cities, but the decision to have a regional or metropolitan mayor was directly negotiated between the government and the 10 Greater Manchester councils Andy Burnham, who left Parliament to stand for the role, was chosen as Labour's candidate for the first mayoral election in 2017 and won, taking 63.4% of the vote

Depending where you are, you might be voting to elect your local council members, a new mayor, members of the Welsh Senedd, or Scottish Parliament But if you're going to vote, you need to make sure you are registered. The deadline for registering to vote has passed but there's still time to apply for a postal or proxy vote Here's what you need to know about how and when you register to vote Registering only takes a few minutes if you do it online. It’s the same process whether you plan to vote in person at a polling station, by post, or through a proxy voter Go to the gov.uk website and fill out the form. You'll need your National Insurance number and current address details to hand If you can't provide your NI number you can still complete the online form but you'll need to provide documents like a passport or utility bill to your local electoral registration office before you're properly registered

Fire crews desperately tried to stop the spread as smog once again smothered Greater Manchester View 6 Ima News James Holt Senior Live and Breaking News Reporter 22:41, 13 Jul 2026 As the third day drew to a close, the moors above Saddleworth were still burning. Yet, the sights of the dried ground violently burning are nothing new. There have already been multiple wildfires in Greater Smog and smoke plumes billowed over the site again as fire crews remained on the scene after first being called out on Saturday Monday was another challenging day for emergency services. Roads remained closed. Strict avoid area warnings were in place. Local residents were urged to keep windows and doors closed Thick plumes of smoke continued to be blown over parts of Greater Manchester, with the stench smelt again as far south at Stockport and Levenshulme . He was believed to have swallowed ‘wraps’ of cocaine found in his underwear , the M.E.N previously reported He was taken to Wythenshawe Hospital where he died of cocaine toxicity, an inquest opening into his death heard last August . An IOPC investigation was launched shortly after and the four officers involved were suspended pending a disciplinary review At a pre-inquest review hearing held at Manchester Coroner’s Court today, senior coroner Zack Golombeck moved the full inquest to begin on February 22 next year - more than two years on from Mr Joyce’s death

title: The plan to build a 14-mile Manchester to Sheffield underground dual carriageway

input.lead: Transport The plan to build a 14-mile Manchester to Sheffield underground dual carriageway Original plans to create the dual carriageway under the Peak District between Manchester and Sheffield was shelved due to cost.
input.summary: Transport The plan to build a 14-mile Manchester to Sheffield underground dual carriageway Original plans to create the dual carriageway under the Peak District between Manchester and Sheffield was shelved due to cost. Now a group thinks they can do i A 14-mile tunnel beneath the
input.evidence_text: Transport The plan to build a 14-mile Manchester to Sheffield underground dual carriageway Original plans to create the dual carriageway under the Peak District between Manchester and Sheffield was shelved due to cost. Now a group thinks they can do i A 14-mile tunnel beneath the Peak District mountains could cut journey times between Manchester and Sheffield by 30 minutes, according to an ambitious proposal. A Norway-inspired dual carriageway would see the snakes of traffic commuting between Manchester and Sheffield removed f The Woodhead railway line between the major northern cities would also be restored under the plans. The fast line between the two major northern cities closed to the public in the 1970s and then entirely in 1981 The scheme, named ‘Trans-Pennine Connect’, aims to make transport between south Yorkshire and the north west better. By putting that traffic underground, they say they can hand the Peak District landscape back to nature This in turn, they say, could boost the productivity of the region and inject millions into the local economy. The construction of the Mottram bypass is currently ongoing, and there is belief Trans-Pennine Connect would link to this scheme and take traffic through the tunnel towa Fresh plans to build the stretch of 14-mile dual carriageway come years after the government shelved a similar scheme – estimating a £10.6bn cost. Now Future Works, a group of infrastructure experts, believe this can be done for just under £2bn

The plot sits near to the Strangeways and Cambridge Strategic Regene The scheme has an estimated construction cost of £70m, according to a viability appraisal by CBRE. input.summary: Fires, fights and football mar a hot weekend in Manchester Dear readers — media outlets local and national are ablaze with news of the Dovestone moor fire. We’re sure that, as with us, many Millers saw and smelt the smoke before they’d seen the fire on the news
input.evidence_text: Fires, fights and football mar a hot weekend in Manchester Dear readers — media outlets local and national are ablaze with news of the Dovestone moor fire. We’re sure that, as with us, many Millers saw and smelt the smoke before they’d seen the fire on the news. We’ll have more on that in just a minute And last but not least for this intro, we of course have our very own Sunday read. Ophira’s article on the figure behind the ‘Salford University Rape Cover Up’ posters that have appeared all across Greater Manchester received a predictably emotional response, given the difficult Are the spectacular plans for Manchester United’s new ground - a 100,000-seater, just 350 metres from the current one - a vanity project? No, says the project’s chief executive Collette Roche, it’s a “ sanity project ” . A year later, we met him in court On the first day of the trial earlier this month, TV historian Laurence Westgaph was asked to turn to page 255 of the hearing bundle: the thick ringbinder of documents that chronicle a year-long legal battle that was now, finally, coming to a head in court Westgaph, a well-known figure in Liverpool who has presented history shows on the BBC, was asked to read a Facebook post he’d published about me in July last year. He began to read it out “I served the big busta, Mr Herrmann this week…” Our barrister, Claire Overman, stopped him. She hadn’t meant for him to read the post out loud. Everyone in court read the Facebook post in silence. “This is just the starter though,” Westgaph had written on top of an image of himself staring at the camera with his shirt unbutton This was the denouement of a tough hour-long cross-examination of Westgaph, in which it often looked like he was struggling to explain why he had initiated this lawsuit in the first place. title: Let's get metaphysical: could an ex-philosopher be the new Burnham?

input.lead: She was one of Greater Manchester's first Green councillors.
input.summary: She was one of Greater Manchester's first Green councillors. Now she has her eyes on a bigger prize
input.evidence_text: She was one of Greater Manchester's first Green councillors. Now she has her eyes on a bigger prize Before politics, one interest dominated the life of Geraldine Coggins, leader of the Trafford Greens, candidate for Greater Manchester mayor, and that was metaphysical nihilism. The now-politician harbours a lifelong love of philosophy, studying the subject up and down the UK and What is metaphysical nihilism? When I ask her, Coggins seems reluctant to discuss it, directing me instead towards the book’s synopsis. “The content is very abstract,” she tells me, “and doesn’t affect my worldview now.” Maybe Coggins is trying to draw a distinction between the r And what a time in the Green Party it’s been. When I meet her, drinking matcha outside a glass-fronted MediaCity cafe, she runs me through the last decade-and-a-half in her Irish lilt: a councillor for eight years, a group leader for four; becoming one of Trafford's first Green c Coggins possesses a demeanour at once warm and authoritative, and it’s this unlikely mix that I’m told helped her win the favour of Altrincham’s electorate in 2018, when, alongside councillor Daniel Jerrome, she made history Two seats were vacant in the ward that year, and the pair stood as the Green candidates. Jerrome recalls a febrile atmosphere on polling day, with neither opposition parties nor the press able to predict what was to come

input.evidence_text: The incident happened on Bradgate Road last month Share on WhatsApp Share via Email A convicted murderer has pleaded guilty to kidnapping a 15-year-old girl at knifepoint and subjecting her to an eight-hour ordeal in his car Paul Sharpe, 56, of Portwood, Stockport, entered guilty pleas at Minshull Street Crown Court on Thursday to six offences: kidnapping, sexual assault, engaging in sexual activity in the presence of a child, making threats with a bladed article, committing an offence with intent to Sharpe approached the girl on Bradgate Road in Altrincham at around 7.30pm on May 18, threatened her with a knife, put her in a headlock and forced her into his car. He told her that her father owed him money He drove her to a car park near Manchester Airport, where he tied her wrists and ankles with a necktie. Over the following hours he moved between different car parks, forcing her to drink alcohol and sexually assaulting her The girl’s mother reported her missing, prompting a major police investigation. title: More than a game: football's role in one family's fostering journey - Rochdale Borough Council

input.lead: A foster family from Heywood have opened up about how their love for football has helped to create the ultimate loving environment for the children in their care
input.summary: A foster family from Heywood have opened up about how their love for football has helped to create the ultimate loving environment for the children in their care
input.evidence_text: A foster family from Heywood have opened up about how their love for football has helped to create the ultimate loving environment for the children in their care Lauren (46) and her husband Danny Sullivan (52) have fostered for Rochdale Borough Council since 2012 and currently care for 5 young lads aged 12 to 21, alongside their 9-year-old son, Daniel Their home is very busy as you can imagine and ‘the washing machine is non-stop’ Lauren jokes. Each child has their own interests, including things like the gym and gaming, but football is what stands out as the cement in their daily routines From training and coaching on weekdays to refereeing and playing matches at the weekend, the house is football mad and it’s their passion for the sport which connects them as a family and what has helped the lads to create a strong brotherly bond “They’ll all come home after school, college or work, and Daniel will ask them for a game,” Lauren said, with a pair of football nets visible outside from the living room “It’s so lovely to see the smiles on their faces when they’re all playing together in the back garden.”

title: New funding will help the council tackle the sticky issue of chewing gum on Rochdale's streets - Rochdale Borough Council

input.lead: Rochdale Borough Council has been awarded a grant from the Chewing Gum Task Force, administered by environmental charity Keep Britain Tidy, to help clean up gum and reduce gum littering

input.evidence_text: Rochdale Borough Council has been awarded a grant from the Chewing Gum Task Force, administered by environmental charity Keep Britain Tidy, to help clean up gum and reduce gum littering Today, the council announced plans to step up the removal of chewing gum that blights Rochdale town centre after receiving a £27,461 grant to tackle the issue Rochdale is one of 50 councils across the country that have successfully applied to the Chewing Gum Task Force, now in its fifth year, for funds to clean gum off pavements and prevent it from being littered in the first place The Chewing Gum Task Force grant scheme – established by Defra (Department for Environment, Food and Rural Affairs) and run by environmental charity Keep Britain Tidy – is open to councils across the UK who wish to clean up gum in their local areas and invest in long-term behavio The Task Force is funded by major gum manufacturers including Mars Wrigley and Perfetti Van Melle, with an investment of up to £10 million spread over 5 years In the past 4 years, the Task Force has awarded grants worth a total of £6.46 million, funded the cleaning of over 4.15 million square metres of pavements

input.evidence_text: Plantation Lodges and Gates have opened following an extensive renovation period, funded by the National Lottery Heritage Fund and Wigan Council The iconic Grade II* listed Lodges and Gates, located on Wigan Lane at the edge of Haigh Woodland Park, have been fully restored over an 18 month period by Wigan Council and were officially opened by Lisa Nandy MP, Secretary of state for culture, media and sport, on Friday, 3rd J Speaking at the event, Ms Nandy said: “This is Wigan, we are friendly, welcoming people who believe these assets are for us all forever. This is more than a beautiful restoration, it’s a bridge between our past, present and future. Places like Haigh bring us together.’ The opening was also attended by The Mayor of Wigan Borough, Councillor Anne Collins, and Councillor Nazia Rehman, Leader of Wigan Council Also in attendance was Councillor Chris Ready, portfolio holder for communities and neighbourhoods. Councillor Ready said: "It’s incredible to have Plantation Gates and Lodges restored and open to residents for the very first time. The reaction to the transformation and the artwo Also in attendance was David Woods, a former resident who lived in the Lodges with his family over 50 years ago. The timetable also means traders will have the opportunity to enjoy Following the decision for the market to be operated directly by Wigan Council, additional work is now being undertaken to focus on developing an operating model that works for the people at the heart of the market This insight is now feeding into the final layout of the building’s internal design, ensuring the market’s long-standing businesses remain at the heart of one of the borough’s most significant regeneration projects Wigan Market forms the centrepiece of the wider Fettlers development and will bring the market back to the heart of Wigan town centre, creating a vibrant destination where people can shop, eat, meet and spend time together

title: Encouraging decline in youth smoking and vaping across Wigan Borough

input.lead: Wigan Borough is seeing a positive decline in the number of young people smoking and regularly vaping, according to a recent Trading Standards survey Following national concerns about youth smoking and vaping, Wigan Council has made it a priority to reduce these figures locally a
input.summary: Wigan Borough is seeing a positive decline in the number of young people smoking and regularly vaping, according to a recent Trading Standards survey Following national concerns about youth smoking and vaping, Wigan Council has made it a priority to reduce these figures locally a
input.evidence_text: Wigan Borough is seeing a positive decline in the number of young people smoking and regularly vaping, according to a recent Trading Standards survey Following national concerns about youth smoking and vaping, Wigan Council has made it a priority to reduce these figures locally and protect the health of our children and young people With more than 1,000 school children aged 14-17 included in the Trading Standards survey, the findings show the number of young people who have tried vaping dropped from around 44 per cent in 2023 to 18 per cent in 2026 The survey, carried out between November 2025 and February 2026, also highlights that smoking is at an all-time low, with around 97 per cent reporting to have never tried smoking Councillor Keith Cunliffe, Deputy Leader of Wigan Council and cabinet portfolio holder for adult social care and health, said: “I’m really pleased to see smoking and vaping rates among young people in Wigan Borough continuing to fall “Reducing tobacco and nicotine use among young people is one of the most effective ways to prevent long-term ill health and reduce health inequalities in our communities

input.evidence_text: The Mayor unveils the new Blue Plaque for Dr John Garton on Park Road, Golborne A renowned Wigan Borough scientist has received a heritage Blue Plaque outside his former home in Golborne Dr John Garton’s (1863–1922) pioneering work in crop manufacturing has had a lasting impact across the globe The Blue Plaque was unveiled at a ceremony on Park Road, Golborne, attended by the Mayor of Wigan Borough Councillor Chris Ready, cabinet portfolio holder for communities and neighbourhoods, said: “Dr John Garton’s work has helped transform farming practices and improve sustainability, making him a significant figure not just locally, but across the world “The Blue Plaque scheme is designed to celebrate the impact residents of our borough have made and Dr Garton’s legacy and influence in the world of agriculture will live on.”

input.evidence_text: Wigan Council will commit £3m to a flood defence scheme that could unlock further major funding Ahead of public consultation this summer, the local authority is hoping its contribution will attract wider support The scheme would see flood risk prevention measures tailored for communities in Hindley, Platt Bridge and Abram, areas recently affected by river flooding The consultation will provide an opportunity to assess the available options and to learn more about how the projects will progress Councillor Nazia Rehman, Leader of Wigan Council, said: “This commitment sets out a clear timeline for how we will support our residents, many of whom have been impacted by recent flooding “We have been consistently making the case on their behalf; listening to residents, working closely with partners such as Defra and the Environment Agency, and lobbying for the funding and support our communities need

input.evidence_text: Inhale Me, Exhale You By Kate Partington Tue 2 Jun 10:00 - Mon 28 Sep 22:00 Art Tue 2 Jun - Mon 28 Sep From 10:00 Tue 2 Jun - Mon 28 Sep From 10:00 Arches Windows No booking required Unspoken feelings and moments of desire You can see this artwork in HOME Arches Windows on Whitwo This piece belongs to a wider series called ‘Desire Me.’ It aims to translate unspoken feelings and moments of desire that occur between us The focus is on longing, on reaching out and on instances of hopeful touch that happen in the early stages of a relationship. title: Marina Abramović: Balkan Erotic Epic | Aviva Studios

input.lead: Experience the raw, unfiltered power of Balkan folklore in a new immersive show from the world’s most famous performance artist Balkan Erotic Epic is a bold new performance work created by the iconic artist Marina Abramović .
input.summary: Experience the raw, unfiltered power of Balkan folklore in a new immersive show from the world’s most famous performance artist Balkan Erotic Epic is a bold new performance work created by the iconic artist Marina Abramović . Regarded as the world’s most influential performance a
input.evidence_text: Experience the raw, unfiltered power of Balkan folklore in a new immersive show from the world’s most famous performance artist Balkan Erotic Epic is a bold new performance work created by the iconic artist Marina Abramović . Regarded as the world’s most influential performance artist, Abramović’s transformative work has reached millions of people – from opening the Pyramid Stage at Glastonbury Festival t A four-hour ritual where ancient myth meets performance art, Balkan Erotic Epic explores the eroticism, spirituality and traditions of Abramović’s homeland through 13 visceral scenes Brought to life by a cast of over 70 performers, dancers, musicians and singers, the experience unfolds across Aviva Studios. Audiences are free to choose their own path – with pop-up encounters punctuating the action, bringing bodies together in dance, song and ritual Watch Fertility Rite , a fevered ritual where bodies writhe against the ground in a desperate call for fertility. Witness Massaging the Breast where women gesticulate over graves to awaken the earth. In Scaring the Gods , women bare themselves to the sky to banish storms. Listen Unafraid to push the boundaries of eroticism and performance art, Balkan Erotic Epic asks its audience a timeless question: What are our bodies truly for?

input.evidence_text: Join this creative workshop for some stitching, chatting, and sharing inspired by the museum’s collections What is the right to strike and is it under threat? input.evidence_text: Bottomless Bracelets Workshop - Manchester’s Finest Bottomless Bracelets Workshop Fri 17th Jul, 2026 - Saan1 Bottomless Bracelets Workshop Saan1, 5 Kelvin Street, Manchester M4 1ET Fri 17th Jul, 2026 6pm From £25 Book tickets Directions events Go to Manchester's Finest Facebook page Go to Manchester's Finest X profile Create as many up-cycled stretch bracelets as you can manage at this beginner-friendly jewellery workshop, using a curated collection of reclaimed beads and materials. input.evidence_text: The Only King is acclaimed as the first original Cantonese biblical musical to present the story of the Book of 1 Samuel in its entirety. The production follows the life of King Saul, from his anointing and rise to the throne to his gradual downfall through pride and disobedience First staged in 2005, The Only King enjoyed multiple sold-out performances in Hong Kong and is widely regarded as a landmark work in Chinese Christian musical theatre To mark the 20th anniversary of its premiere, The Only King was presented in London and Cambridge in January 2026, where it received enthusiastic responses and strong audience acclaim. title: Спектакль "Скамейка"

input.lead: Друзья, а вы давно ощущали настоящую магию театра?
input.summary: Друзья, а вы давно ощущали настоящую магию театра? 29 ноября 2026 года в Logan Hall — легендарная «Скамейка» с Алексеем Паниным
input.evidence_text: Спектакль «Скамейка» в Лондоне | Алексей Панин | 29 ноября 2026 :: EventFirst Home double_arrow Events double_arrow Спектакль "Скамейка" On sale Спектакль "Скамейка" schedule 29 Nov 2026 Sunday, 19:00 location_on Logan Hall 20 Bedford Way, London WC1H 0AL balcony 6:00 pm gpp_maybe NO REFUNDS Buy On MAP Buy Tickets Logan Hall £30.00 Select Logan Hall £35.00 Select Logan Hall £40.00 Select Logan Hall £45.00 Select Logan Hall £50.00 Select Logan Hall £55.00 Select Logan Hall £60.00 Select Logan Hall £65.00 Sold Out Logan Hall £70.00 Select Logan Hall £80.00 Select Logan Hall £85.00 Sold Out Logan Hall £90.00 Select Друзья, а вы давно ощущали настоящую магию театра? 29 ноября 2026 года в Logan Hall — легендарная «Скамейка» с Алексеем Паниным. После аншлагов в Европе и США спектакль впервые приезжает в Лондон. 📍 University College London, Level 1, 20 Bedford Way, London WC1H 0AL 🕕 DOORS: 18:00 🕖 SHOW: 19:00 «Скамейка» — знаменитая пьеса, когда-то блистательно сыгранная Олегом Ефремовым и Татьяной Дорониной на сцене МХАТа, а затем покорившая театральные площадки по всему миру. Это история о мужчине и женщине. О случайной встрече, которая постепенно превращается в откровенный разговор — честный, смешной, ранимый и очень человеческий. О том моменте, когда между людьми исчезают роли и, наконец, становится невозможно притворяться. На сцене — Алексей Панин и Ольга Силина. Тонкая актерская игра, живая энергия и удивительно узнаваемая история, в которой каждый услышит что-то своё. “Скамейка” — спектакль, после которого хочется немного помолчать и еще долго думать о главном. Если вам не хватает настоящего театра — этот вечер нельзя пропустить. Количество билетов ограничено. Продолжительность спектакля — 2 часа с антрактом. settings Loading venue plan... shopping_cart Your tickets: 0

input.evidence_text: Porta and Tartuffe have spent months building a 10 course Spanish-inspired menu of Gildas, grilled seafood, chicken thighs and giant open-fire paella By Manchester's Finest, Last updated 9 July 2026 Go to Manchester's Finest X profile There are few restaurants that inspire the kind of loyalty Porta does. The sort of place where you go in for a vermouth and a couple of plates, then realise two hours later that you’ve ordered half the menu and are seriously considering another That easy-going, no-fuss approach has made the walk-in-only tapas bar a favourite across its four sites in Chester, Altrincham, Salford and West Didsbury. Founded by brothers Ben and Joe Wright, Porta takes its cues from the neighbourhood bars of Spain: short menus, good produce, For the next Open Fire Dining Club , that Spanish influence is being taken out of Porta’s dining rooms and into the open air at Torr Vale Mill. input.evidence_text: Beyond the quick answer - How to get real work out of Claude and your business data with Tugger | Manchester Digital Beyond the quick answer - How to get real work out of Claude and your business data with Tugger View Profile View More Events 15 July 2026 11:00 - 12:00 Book Now Your data's full of answers. The hard part is getting at them. If you run your business across more than one system, you'll know the feeling. Your numbers are in your accounts, your customers are in your CRM, your sales and stock are somewhere else again. Getting a straight answer usually means exporting from each system and stitching it together by hand. There's a better way, and it goes well beyond asking a quick question. In this session, Craig Morrall, co-founder of Tugger, will show you how to get Claude doing real work with your business data. Not one-line answers, but full reports, dashboards, and reporting that runs itself. Four things most people don't know Claude can do: Pull two systems together in one question. Ask something that spans your accounts and your CRM at once and get one clear answer, the thing no single app can do. Build a full board pack in minutes. Watch Claude pull the numbers, write the commentary, and hand you a finished document you'd take to the board. Spin up a dashboard you can interact with. An interactive dashboard built from a single prompt. No Power BI skills, no formulas, no faff. Put your reporting on autopilot. Set it up once so the routine stuff, like the Monday morning report, gets done before anyone sits down. This is made for any business running on more than one system, whatever your sector. Already a Tugger customer? You'll pick up plenty to get more from what you've already got. New to us? You'll see exactly what's possible with your own data. Book N

input.evidence_text: Moneybox to become unicorn in secondary share sale Posted on July 13, 2026 by Jonathan Symcox Moneybox to become unicorn in secondary share sale Employees to cash in as FinTech prepares for transaction on London Stock Exchange’s Private Securities Market which will value it at £800m Share: FinTech Deals Investment Digital wealth management platform Moneybox has today announced that it is preparing for a secondary share sale worth up to £45 million for long-serving employees that will value the company at around £800m. It marks an increase of around 45% since 2024. In 2025, Moneybox exceeded £115m in annual revenue and delivered its third consecutive year of profitability. This momentum has continued into 2026 with more than 390,000 new customers joining the platform so far this year and £3.5 billion in net inflows in the first half of the year. The firm says the increased valuation reflects the company’s continued growth, operational maturity and long term potential as increasing numbers of customers choose Moneybox to support them across every stage of their financial lives, from saving and investing to home-buying and retirement. In the decade since it first launched, Moneybox has evolved from a simple saving and investing app to one of the UK’s fastest growing wealth management platforms, supporting more than 1.9m customers with over £23bn in assets under administration. Along the way, the company has helped more than 200,000 customers save for their first home, with a customer using a Moneybox Lifetime ISA to complete a home purchase every 10 minutes during 2025. Services operating on the East Didsbury and Manchester Airport lines will terminate at Firswood Replacement bus services will operate throughout the closure period to help people complete their journeys. People who normally use the affected lines are being encouraged to check the Bee Network app or website before they travel. Eat, meet, drink, shop, watch, talk, listen, laugh. Come in, be yourself and if you like it bring your friends! Altrincham’s charter dates back to 1290, it’s location at the intersection of the Cheshire farm belt and the commercial commuter zone of Manchester means the town has a long and important history. Altrincham market is now at the heart of the regeneration of ‘the modern market tow The covered market hosts regional food producers, vintage fashion and furniture, home & garden and contemporary craft on a Tuesday, Friday, Saturday and Sunday Altrincham Market House Opening Hours Tues-Sat : 9am-10pm Sun : 9am-6pm Mon : Closed The Outdoor Market (Shopping) Tues: 8am-3pm Fri: 8am-3pm Sat: 8am-4pm Sun: 10am-4pm Altrincham Market House is home to award-winning independent quality food operators. title: Bolton Car Boot Sale

input.lead: Bolton Car Boot Sale at Bolton Car Boot Sale
input.summary: Huge Sunday car boot at Bolton's Macron Stadium (BL6 6JW), Feb-Dec. 300+ stalls, £1 per car entry, catering, toilets and dog-friendly Event date: 2026-07-19 Venue: Bolton Car Boot Sale Price: £1 Booking: https://carbootdirectory.co.uk:2053/listings/bolton-car-boot/ Status: https:
input.evidence_text: Bolton Car Boot Sale | Bolton Sunday Car Boot Sale | Times, Prices & Info Skip to content 210+ UK car boot sales — find one near you this weekend Type your postcode or town to find sales near you Search car boot sales About This Car Boot Sale Bolton Car Boot Sale is one of the North West's biggest weekly boot sales, held in the hardstanding car park of the Macron Stadium (formerly Reebok), Burnden Way, Horwich, Bolton BL6 6JW. It runs every Sunday from February right through to December, drawing over 300 sellers and thousands of bargain hunters each week. Sellers can arrive and set up from 6:00 AM, with buyers welcomed in from 7:00 AM. Entry costs just £1 per car for buyers. Pitches for sellers are £14 for a car, £20 for a van and £3 for a trailer, with regular and corner pitches available on request. Bring exact change to keep the queue moving, and call organiser Andrew on 07470784644 for more details. Expect hundreds of stalls across the concrete hardstanding pitches, so it goes ahead in most weather. On-site you'll find a 5-star catering service, doughnuts and coffee, ice cream and desserts, a meat auction, fresh fruit and veg, plants, and retro sweet stalls. The sale is dog-friendly, has male and female toilets, ample parking, and kids' rides and bouncy castles to keep little ones entertained. Last confirmed open: 8 June 2026. input.summary: A ghost story by Tim Foley · Directed by Neil Bettles Following a sell-out UK tour and London run, the award-winning ThickSkin present this chilling new play by Tim Foley (former Bruntwood Prize-winner) When an out-of-work actor takes a job playing a ghost at an old countryside m
input.evidence_text: A ghost story by Tim Foley · Directed by Neil Bettles Following a sell-out UK tour and London run, the award-winning ThickSkin present this chilling new play by Tim Foley (former Bruntwood Prize-winner) When an out-of-work actor takes a job playing a ghost at an old countryside manor, he expects nothing more than a few cheap scares. But as night after night unfolds, the role begins to blur with reality — and something far more sinister starts to emerge Part ghost story, part psychological thriller, It Walks Around The House At Night draws audiences into a world of creeping dread, unexpected twists and dark humour. A haunted house story for a new era, brought to life through inventive staging, immersive sound and bold visual eff ThickSkin, one of the UK’s most exciting touring companies, joins forces with Tim Foley (award-winning writer of Doctor Who audio dramas; Electric Rosary, Royal Exchange Theatre) to create this thrilling production. Directed by Neil Bettles (ThickSkin’s How Not To Drown, Disney’s Unsettling, darkly comic and full of surprises, the stage is set… for terror! Age Recommendation: 14+ Content Warnings: Loud and sudden noises, haze, strobe, flashing lights, occasional strong language, horror imagery and jump scares. Runtime: 90 minutes (no interval)

title: World Cup: England skipper Kane hails Haaland

input.lead: World Cup: England skipper Kane hails Haaland
input.summary: That’s according to England talisman Harry Kane with the striking superstars set to face off tonight in a thrilling World Cup quarter-final. Haaland will lead the line for Norway with Kane set to skipper England in the 22:00 (UK) knockout clash at Miami Stadium
input.evidence_text: That’s according to England talisman Harry Kane with the striking superstars set to face off tonight in a thrilling World Cup quarter-final. Haaland will lead the line for Norway with Kane set to skipper England in the 22:00 (UK) knockout clash at Miami Stadium. It’s a battle between two of the world’s most prodigious frontmen with not only progress to the last four up for grabs but also the competition’s Golden Boot in the balance with Kane on six and Haaland on seven behind only Argentina’s Lionel Messi and France’s Kylian Mbappe on eight in the fight for the prestigious individual gong. Heading into this evening’s hotly-anticipated encounter, Kane waxed lyrical about his opposite number. “Erling has been incredible,” said Kane. “Look at his goal scoring record. And physically, he’s a machine, he’s a beast. “His finishing is at another level and his goalscoring record speaks for itself. “I respect him a lot as a player and as a fellow professional. “I’m obviously hoping he has a quiet day. His performances speak for themselves. He’s a fantastic player.” The performances of both prolific forwards have led to comparisons and naturally questions of which player is better. It’s a discussion Kane refused to get drawn into primarily because he believes they’re different type of strikers. “It’s an impossible one for me to answer’” he added. “I think we are completely different players. I know we are both seen as strikers but we are almost two different positions if I’m totally honest.” City Magazine: World Cup special available now! Featuring Rayan Cherki, Erling Haaland, Omar Marmoush and a greatest ever World Cup Manchester Best XI - get reading now!

title: Андрей Кравченко

input.lead: Андрей Кравченко at Brixton Jamm
input.summary: Андрей Кравченко в Лондоне! 17 октября 2026 года в Brixton Jamm состоится концерт Андрея Кравченко в Лондоне
input.evidence_text: 17 октября 2026 года в Brixton Jamm состоится концерт Андрея Кравченко в Лондоне Андрей Кравченко покорил сердца тысяч слушателей благодаря своему уникальному голосу, харизме и способности превращать личные истории в песни, близкие каждому. В его творчестве смешиваются романтическая лирика, современное эстрадное звучание и проникновенная искренность После серии аншлаговых выступлений на самых престижных сценах Украины, среди которых Жовтневый Дворец и Дворец “Украина”, певец продолжает свое концертное путешествие и приглашает поклонников на особую встречу в Лондоне Концерты Андрея Кравченко известны своей теплой атмосферой: здесь нет дистанции между сценой и залом, ведь каждое выступление превращается в откровенный разговор на языке музыки. Гостей ждут любимые хиты, живой звук, море эмоций и моменты, которые захочется сохранить в памяти над Важной частью творческой миссии артиста остается поддержка Украины — часть средств от концерта будет передана на помощь ВСУ! Подарите себе вечер настоящей романтики, красивой музыки и искренних чувств вместе с Андреем Кравченко

final_reason: Без новых фактов: уже был 2026-06-26 как «Андрей Кравченко».

Презентація максі-синглу "Живий" Кожного разу, коли перед менеджментом гурту Бумбокс стоїть завдання написати пресреліз, ми починаємо згадувати цікаві факти. Скільки концертів зіграли, скільки грошей зібрали, які пісні видали. Це база. Непогано ще пам’ятати, чи був гурт у країні, до якої ми збираємося, чи це пер О, як би хотілося написати: "Концерт гурту Бумбокс" — і як у Львові за добу продано 8000 квитків. Так-так, ми не про Львів розповідаємо, хоча теж про Європу Наші віддані слухачі, якщо ви перебуваєте за кордоном, сподіваємось, вам буде цікаво дізнатися, що після традиційної перерви на декілька місяців, Бумбокс поїде в турне від Ізраїлю до Німеччини, від Англії до Польщі, від Ірландії до Словаччини та Чехії І повеземо ми в це турне свіженький, як херсонський кавун, максі-сингл “Живий”, підсилений піснями з різних альбомів, під частину яких виросли ви, а під частину зростають ваші діти Тож ознайомтеся, будь ласка, уважно з розкладом туру, купуйте квитки заздалегідь, готуйте гроші на донати та мерч — зустрінемося в листопаді

title: Manchester-Sheffield tunnel plans: How Norway-style transport vision could slash travel times between Yorkshire and the North West

input.lead: Economic Development Yorkshire & Humber Manchester-Sheffield tunnel plans: How Norway-style transport vision could slash travel times between Yorkshire and the North West Project backers say it would boost local economies and improve Peak District environment Economic Development
input.summary: Economic Development Yorkshire & Humber Manchester-Sheffield tunnel plans: How Norway-style transport vision could slash travel times between Yorkshire and the North West Project backers say it would boost local economies and improve Peak District environment Economic Development
input.evidence_text: Economic Development Yorkshire & Humber Manchester-Sheffield tunnel plans: How Norway-style transport vision could slash travel times between Yorkshire and the North West Project backers say it would boost local economies and improve Peak District environment Economic Development A 14-mile tunnel beneath the Peak District mountains could cut journey times between Manchester and Sheffield by 30 minutes, according to an ambitious proposal A Norway-inspired dual carriageway would see the snakes of traffic commuting between Manchester and Sheffield removed from the national park, and sent underground instead The Woodhead railway line between the major northern cities would also be restored under the plans. The fast line between the two major northern cities closed to the public in the 1970s and then entirely in 1981 This scheme, named 'Trans-Pennine Connect', aims to make transport between south Yorkshire and the north west better. By putting that traffic underground, they say they can hand the Peak District landscape back to nature This in turn, they say, could boost the productivity of the region and inject millions into the local economy. The construction of the Mottram bypass is currently ongoing, there is belief Trans-Pennine Connect would link to this scheme and take traffic through the tunnel towards

But the plan goes far beyond that Trafford's Wharfside Regeneration Masterplan promises around 15,000 new homes, 48,000 new jobs, a possible new train station, green spaces and places for a new community to come together to eat, drink, shop and play Council leader Tom Ross wants to transform the land around the Old Trafford football grounds into a place where new and existing residents and businesses can thrive. input.evidence_text: Economic Development Manchester United confirms new stadium location and plans for 'world-class' arena New 100,000-seater home will be some 350 metres away from Old Trafford Economic Development Tyrone Marshall Senior Manchester United Football Writer 08:32, 10 Jul 2026 Updated 0 Manchester United have confirmed the club's new 100,000-seater stadium will be built on land recently purchased from Indurent, situated roughly 350 metres from Old Trafford United confirmed the location for the new stadium on Thursday in the Wharfside Strategic Masterplan and while the stadium won't be built on land currently connected to Old Trafford, they insist it will honour the current ground's history and traditions The new ground will be the centrepiece of a Stadium District, which will be purpose-built for sport, entertainment and year-round activity, with the new stadium serving as the flagship landmark of the wider Trafford Wharfside development The masterplan sets out a bold vision for Trafford Wharfside, including new and improved public transport links, enhanced rail connectivity, and extensive walking and cycling infrastructure. The vision is for a diverse neighbourhood creating 48,000 local jobs and 15,000 new homes It is estimated it could offer a £7.3bn per year boost to the UK economy, reports the Manchester Evening News

input.evidence_text: Danish-born juice bar and coffee concept JOE & THE JUICE is bringing its signature juices, coffee, and cult-favourite sandwiches to Manchester this summer with the launch of Sunlight House The opening of Sunlight House marks a major expansion for the juice bar in Manchester, continuing its growth across the city following the launch of its Mosley Street store last month and ahead of a new Oxford Road opening later this summer To celebrate launch day on Friday 10th July at, JOE & THE JUICE will be treating the first 100 customers through the doors to a free juice Designed with JOE & THE JUICE’s modern Scandinavian heritage in mind, the store will feature the brand’s instantly recognisable open counter layout, minimalist interiors and natural materials. The space will also integrate the JOE app experience, allowing customers to pre-order, The Sunlight House menu will showcase JOE’s full range of freshly prepared, functional offerings. Guests can enjoy its iconic juices and wellness shots, alongside protein shakes, specialty coffee and matcha drinks, as well as its popular Scandi-style sandwiches made fresh using t JOE & THE JUICE Sunlight House will open on Friday 10th July 2026, from 9am
